learning_outcomes.description: Understand professional legal practice through engaging directly in legally-oriented work.
Understand and critically appreciate the role and value of lawyering in the context of the organisation where the internship is completed.
Understand the practice dimensions of the particular legal area relevant to the internship.
Have greater insight into their own learning and professional identity.
Bring critical insight into some of the challenges and opportunities facing lawyers and/or organisations and the social, financial, and legal frameworks within which they operate.
A deeper understanding of, and increased capacity to engage with, law, policy and reform in the area of the internship.

assessments.assessment_title: Attendance at Placement (120 hours) AND Attendance at Teaching Workshops and Check-in Sessions
assessments.task_description: Attendance is an inherent requirement of this course. Students are expected to attend their placement for a total of 150 hours throughout the semester, including any induction or debriefing sessions required by their supervising organisation. Attendance will be verified with supervising organisations. Students are also required to attend and participate in three (3) x 2 hour in-person teaching workshops held throughout semester. These workshops will be held in Week 2, Week 6, and Week 13 of semester - exact time and location TBC (Details will be provided on Blackboard).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students are required to complete a Student Learning Plan which provides details of their placement arrangements, an overview of relevant tasks and activities they anticipate being involved with during the placement, and a brief reflection on their learning goals for the semester arising from the placement opportunity. A template will be provided on Blackboard for students to complete and this can also be used as a basis for discussion with placement supervisors, as appropriate.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Learning Plans must be submitted electronically via the submission link on the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Students are required to submit a reflective journal and give an accompanying 5-minute talk during Workshop 2 (in week 13 of semester). The reflective journal and presentation should cover a range of topics, including an assessment of the placement experience against their original learning plan, a reflection on activities/insights gained from each of the teaching workshops, and a thoughtful review of how the placement experience developed their understanding of professional practice and their own career goals and development. A detailed task sheet with prompt questions will be available to students on the Blackboard site to guide the oral presentation and journal responses.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Reflective Journals must be submitted electronically via the submission link on the course Blackboard site. Presentations will take place during Workshop 2 in Week 13.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom O-week To Week 13(20 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Placement (120 hours)

Students are required to attend their placement and engage with required tasks and activities, as agreed with supervising organisation, for total of 120 hours through semester.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Placement (120 hours) Students are required to attend their placement and engage with required tasks and activities, as agreed with supervising organisation, for total of 120 hours through semester.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop One: Reflecting on Career Pathways and Preparing for the Workplace

Workshop One will be held in Week 2 of semester (exact time and place TBC). Attendance is required and must be in-person. If exceptional circumstances arise that prevent you from attending, you must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible and provide appropriate documentation in support (e.g. a medical certificate). The 2-hour workshop will cover a range of topics and activities designed to help you finalise your Learning Plan, prepare for your placement, and critically reflect on your learning goals and on the development of your professional skills.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04
learning_activities.topic_title: Workshop One: Reflecting on Career Pathways and Preparing for the Workplace
learning_activities.topic_description: Workshop One: Reflecting on Career Pathways and Preparing for the Workplace Workshop One will be held in Week 2 of semester (exact time and place TBC). Attendance is required and must be in-person. If exceptional circumstances arise that prevent you from attending, you must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible and provide appropriate documentation in support (e.g. a medical certificate). The 2-hour workshop will cover a range of topics and activities designed to help you finalise your Learning Plan, prepare for your placement, and critically reflect on your learning goals and on the development of your professional skills.

learning_activities.topic:
Mid-semester check-in: Developing as a Professional

A mid-semester check-in with your Course Coordinator will be scheduled in Week 6 of semester (exact time and place TBC). The check-in is designed to assist you to reflect on your development to date, with a particular focus on topics such as managing time, communicating through conflict, working in teams, and reflecting on the ethical and social contexts within which lawyering takes place.

Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Mid-semester check-in: Developing as a Professional A mid-semester check-in with your Course Coordinator will be scheduled in Week 6 of semester (exact time and place TBC). The check-in is designed to assist you to reflect on your development to date, with a particular focus on topics such as managing time, communicating through conflict, working in teams, and reflecting on the ethical and social contexts within which lawyering takes place.

learning_activities.topic:
Workshop Two: Reflecting on the Placement Experience and Revisiting Career Pathways

Workshop Two will be held in Week 13 of semester (exact time and place TBC). Attendance is required and must be in-person. If exceptional circumstances arise that prevent you from attending, you must contact the Course Coordinator as soon as possible and provide appropriate documentation in support (e.g. a medical certificate). The 2-hour workshop is designed to assist you to reflect on your placement experience and to use your insights to plan your future professional development and career pathway. You will be required to present your 5-min oral reflection during this Workshop (see assessment items above).

Learning outcomes: L02, L04, L05, L06
